mechanism_narrative: Sustained growth in industrial production (INDPRO) reflects expanding manufacturing output and supply capacity, which boosts corporate earnings and drives positive S&P 500 forward returns over a quarterly horizon.
failure_condition: INDPRO rises over 6 months but SPX forward 60-day returns are negative in more than 50% of such periods over a 5-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: When the federal funds rate drops below 2.0% after having been above 2.5%, it signals a definitive easing cycle that lowers discount rates and raises equity valuations, producing positive 20-day forward returns.
failure_condition: Rank IC over all triggered instances is negative or less than 0.05 over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A simultaneous rise in retail sales (RSAFS) and fall in unemployment (UNRATE) indicates robust consumer demand and tightening labor markets, signaling strong corporate revenue and earnings that lift equities over the next month.
failure_condition: When RSAFS is up and UNRATE is down simultaneously, SPX forward 20-day returns are negative in more than 55% of months over a 3-year period.

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate exceeds 5% and has risen for at least three consecutive months, it signifies recessionary labor slack that compresses corporate earnings and depresses equity prices over the next quarter.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE > 5% and UNRATE > UNRATE 3 months ago
failure_condition: Positive forward 60-day returns occur in more than 40% of periods meeting the trigger condition over any 5-year window.

mechanism_narrative: A monthly housing starts level below 1.2 million units signals a pronounced downturn in residential investment, a leading indicator of economic contraction that drives negative S&P 500 returns over the following month.
failure_condition: When HOUST is below 1200, 20-day forward returns are positive more than 50% of the time over a 10-year backtest.

mechanism_narrative: When CPI inflation exceeds 2.5% year-over-year and the federal funds rate is lower than that inflation rate by at least 50 basis points (i.e., negative real rates), monetary accommodation is extreme, historically boosting equities over a 20-day horizon.
candidate_trigger: 12-month change in CPIAUCSL > 2.5% and FEDFUNDS < (12-month CPIAUCSL change - 0.5)
regime_hint: accommodative monetary policy with rising inflation
failure_condition: When both conditions hold, the mean 20-day forward return is negative or zero over a 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Strong consumer spending growth, as measured by real retail sales (RSAFS), directly boosts corporate revenues and leads to positive short-term equity returns within the following week.
failure_condition: A regression of 5-day forward returns on RSAFS month-over-month change yields a negative or insignificant coefficient over any 3-year period.

mechanism_narrative: Two consecutive monthly declines in industrial production signal a supply-side contraction that foreshadows economic weakness and depresses equity prices over the following month.
candidate_trigger: INDPRO declines month-over-month for two consecutive months
failure_condition: If after two consecutive INDPRO declines the 20-day forward return is positive more than 55% of the time over a 10-year test, the hypothesis fails.
